zh.lifeSummary: 當Marguerite De Bearn於1245年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰培列內-阿特藍可斯保羅時，她的父親Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn24歲，母親Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre17歲。她於1262年10月4日與Roger Bernard III De Foix在法國努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛萊拉可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1319年3月19日在法國奧其坦尼阿利艾福瓦享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marguerite De Bearn于1245年出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰培列內-阿特藍可斯保羅时，她的父亲Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn24岁，母亲Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre17岁。她于1262年10月4日与Roger Bernard III De Foix在法国努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛萊拉可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1319年3月19日在法国奧其坦尼阿利艾福瓦享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bearn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname for a bearded man (from Middle English berd, Old English beard). To be clean-shaven was the norm in non-Jewish communities in northwestern Europe from the 12th to the 16th century, the crucial period for surname formation. There is a placename and other evidence to show that this word was used as a byname in the Old English period, when beards were the norm; in this period the byname would have referred to a large or noticeable beard. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, in particular German Barth 1.

habitational name from a place called Beard in Derbyshire (now represented by Beard Hall and Beardwood Farms in New Mills parish), which derives its name by dissimilation from Old English brerd ‘rim, bank’.

variants.name: Bardwell
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bardwell in Suffolk, so named with an unattested Old English byname Bearda, a derivative of beard (see Beard ) + Old English well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Alternatively, the first element may be from a dissimilated form of Old English bre(o)rd ‘brim, bank’.

variants.name: Barth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Gunther, Klaus, Manfred, Armin, Arno.

German and Swiss German: nickname for a bearded man, from Middle High German bart ‘beard’. This name, which can not be always distinguished from 2 and 4 below, is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia, in the last four countries sometimes as a German translation of Slavic surnames meaning ‘beard’ or ‘bearded man’ (see Bart 1). See also Beard 1.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Pomerania.

variants.name: Bard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Aime, Alphonse, Alphy, Armand, Cecile, Celestine, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Jacques.

English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Old French personal name Bard(e), ancient Germanic Bardo, from barta ‘battle axe’. This was borne as a surname by a prominent Norman family with lands in west Normandy and in various English and Scottish counties, including Essex, Hertfordshire, North Yorkshire, Northumberland, and Lanarkshire. In Scotland the original family were Norman landowners in Strathaven parish in Lanarkshire. They were descended from the Baards, lords of Loftus in the North Yorkshire, descendants of the mid 12th-century Richard Baart.
Irish: altered form of either   Beard   or   Baird  .

variants.name: Bearb
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aurelien, Camille, Micheline, Pierre.
Altered form of English Beard , under the influence, in LA, of French barbe ‘beard’.

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite De Bearn was born in 1245, in Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, her father, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, was 24 and her mother, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, was 17. She married Roger Bernard III De Foix on 4 October 1262, in Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died on 19 March 1319, in Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, France, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marguerite De Bearn est née en 1245, à Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Roger Bernard III De Foix le 4 octobre 1262, à Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 mars 1319, à Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, France, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marguerite De Bearn 1245 in Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn 24 und ihre Mutter Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre 17. Sie hat am 4. Oktober 1262 in Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich Roger Bernard III De Foix geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 19. März 1319 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite De Bearn è nata il nel 1245 nel Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, sua padre, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Roger Bernard III De Foix il 4 ottobre 1262, nel Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 19 marzo 1319, nel Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, Francia, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite De Bearnが1245年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインプイルeンeエス-アトランティクスプアウで生まれた時，父Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarnは24歳，母Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorreは17歳であった。1262年10月4日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインロト-エト-ガーオンレイラックでRoger Bernard III De Foixと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1319年3月19日にフランスオクシタニーアリeッジフォイックスで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite De Bearn(이)가 1245년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 p이ee스-앳랜티크스 파에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1262년 10월 4일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 로트-트-가니 레이라크에서 Roger Bernard III De Foix(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1319년 3월 19일 프랑스 옥시탠이 애리e지 포이스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite De Bearn nasceu em 1245, em Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger Bernard III De Foix em 4 de outubro de 1262, em Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 19 de março de 1319, em Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, França, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marguerite De Bearn родилась в 1245 г. в местности пау, пирeнeес-атлантикуесь, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, её отцу по имени Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger Bernard III De Foix 4 октября 1262 г. в местности леирак, лот-ет-гаронн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла 19 марта 1319 г. в местности фоикс, ариeдж, окситани, Франция в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marguerite De Bearn nació en 1245, en Pau, Pyrénées-Atlantiques, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Gaston VII Vicomte de Béarn, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Viscomtessa de Marsan Mathe de Marsan De Bigorre, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Roger Bernard III De Foix el 4 de octubre de 1262, en Layrac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 19 de marzo de 1319, en Foix, Ariège, Occitanie, Francia, a la edad de 74 años.
